title: Cytotoxicity BioAssay of human  iPSC-derived Dopamine Neurons (DANs)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"pqmj","text":"This protocol details a sensitive, fast, high-throughput and non-destructive cytolysis assay that measures adenylate kinase (AK) release from damaged cells. It detects AK in cell culture supernatants, providing real-time, kinetic analysis of cell death without requiring cell lysis of iPSCs-derived DANs plated in a 96-well format plate.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bc4p6","text":"Cells should be plated at a density of 50 000 cells/ well in a 96 full area plate which has been precoated with Geltrex. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6s59","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8c8o","text":"Additionally, care should be taken when adding and removing liquid as these adherent cells are prone to peeling. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9qil9","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"19h0g","text":"Avoid exposure to light by covering the plate with aluminium foil.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ccm7","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"at9ns","text":"Record the record within 5 minutes of adding the reagent to the supernatant.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Add 2 mL of Triton X-100 solution in 10 mL PBS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":50}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6cpnk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgv6u","text":"Prepararing Detection Reagent:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30drf","text":"1. Reconstitute the lyophilised AK detection reagent by adding 10 mL of AK assay buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":88}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1s3r3","text":"2. Allow it to sit for 15 mins before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Collect 5 μl of cell supernatant and transfer to a 384-well Greiner LUMITRAC™ plate.
Add 25 μl of detection reagent to each well and keep it for 5 minutes.
Record luminescence was using a PHERAstar® microplate reader.
For positive control, add an equal volume of 20% Triton X-100 solution to the media of one well per cell line for 5 minutes before supernatant collection.
creator: Cláudia C. Mendes
